description:
You are a computational pharmacologist analyzing why imatinib selectively inhibits ABL kinase over related kinases EGFR and CDK2. Load ABL with imatinib (PDB: 1IEP), EGFR with erlotinib (PDB: 1M17), and CDK2 with Cyclin A (PDB: 1FIN).

Structurally superimpose all three kinase domains and record pairwise RMSD values.

Identify the drug binding pocket in each kinase: select all protein residues within 5 Angstroms of the ligand for ABL (ligand residue name STI) and EGFR (ligand residue name AQ4), or within 5 Angstroms of the catalytic lysine for CDK2.

Perform a binding pocket conservation analysis: for each ABL pocket residue, use the structural alignment to find the equivalent position in EGFR and CDK2, compare amino acid types, and classify each position as conserved (identical amino acid in all three kinases) or divergent. Color conserved pocket residues on ABL in blue and divergent residues in red.

Identify the gatekeeper residue and DFG motif conformation (DFG-in or DFG-out) in each kinase. Measure the CA-CA distance from gatekeeper to DFG-phenylalanine, and the catalytic lysine NZ to C-helix glutamate OE1 distance, in each structure.

Save a ray-traced figure at 2400x1800 resolution to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/kinase_selectivity.png showing all three superimposed kinases with conservation-colored pockets, DFG motifs as sticks, and ligands as spheres.

Write a report to ~/PyMOL_Data/kinase_selectivity_report.txt with: pairwise RMSDs, each pocket residue's conservation status and equivalent amino acids, gatekeeper and DFG analysis, all distances, and a structural rationale for imatinib's ABL selectivity.
